answer:
\boxed{D}  

English courts have discretion to stay proceedings due to abuse of process in cases of extra-territorial abduction, but such stays are granted only in exceptional circumstances. The leading case *R v Horseferry Road Magistrates’ Court, ex p Bennett* established that while the court may refuse jurisdiction, it will seldom do so unless the abduction involves serious state misconduct. Thus, option D best reflects the current legal position.
